Description: Biological sex or gender identity — used to tailor programme recommendations
Age in years — used to adjust intensity and recovery expectations
Current body weight in kilograms — used as a baseline for tracking progress
Height in centimetres — used to calculate BMI and inform programme design
The participant's fitness goals, e.g. lose weight, build strength, run a 5K — drives programme design
Known injuries, pain points, or physical limitations the agent must respect when designing exercises
Equipment available, preferred workout days, exercise style preferences, or anything else relevant to personalisation
